summary: High Time shines, The Weight > Hey Pocky Way sequence electrifies
blurb: The set opens with a punchy Cold Rain And Snow and builds through rare gems like High Time. The second set delivers a scorching run highlighted by The Weight, Foolish Heart and an explosive Hey Pocky Way climax. Brent’s keyboards and the twin drummers lock in tight throughout.
review: On March 28 1990 at Nassau Coliseum the Dead turned in a spring‑time blaze that still feels fresh. An energetic Cold Rain And Snow kicks off the evening, followed by a first set packed with seldom‑heard moments – High Time and Loose Lucy sparkle while Jerry’s vocal fire on Deal lifts the mood. The real fireworks erupt in Set 2: Foolish Heart rolls into a seamless The Weight → Hey Pocky Way transition that rockets into a wild The Other One/Wharf Rat/G​ood Lovin' suite, all underscored by Brent Mydland’s unapologetically honest keyboard work (including his infamous profane interjection) and the twin‑drum engine of Mickey and Bill driving the groove. Across the board reviewers rave about the crystal‑clear Miller soundboard capture, noting soaring leads on Cumberland Blues and a tight, adventurous flow that makes this night one of the season’s standouts.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All ten detailed recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising High Time, The Weight, Foolish Heart and the tight band interplay while no consistent negative comments appear about performance. Multiple sources highlight Brent’s keyboards, Jerry’s soaring leads and the second‑set flow, yielding a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear Miller soundboard with excellent mix, full set captured and praised as the clearest representation of the night’s performance.

key_highlights: Scorching second set with The Weight → Hey Pocky Way → The Other One/Wharf Rat climax
High Time shines early in Set 1 and sets a high‑energy tone
Brent Mydland delivers standout, honest keyboard work (including a memorable profane moment)
Cold Rain And Snow opens the show with punch and drive

band_performance.Jerry: Powerful vocals on Deal and soaring leads on Cumberland Blues; intense, ‘mad’ solos throughout the second set keep the energy high.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation, especially prominent during Cumberland Blues and the peak of the second‑set jam; overall steady presence despite a single note of being less front‑and‑center.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers warm vocal work on ballads like Easy To Love You, High Time and Queen Jane Approximately, adding spacey guitar textures later in the set.
band_performance.Brent: Shines with memorable keyboard lines, honest tone, and even a cheeky profane moment that became talk of the night; his playing drives the improvisational flow.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides tight, driving rhythm; highlighted in the pre‑drum segment and maintains steady pulse throughout the marathon second set.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in with Mickey for a solid groove, adds an Appalachian feel on Cumberland Blues and keeps the drums powerful and focused during the high‑energy peaks.
